commands.review_summary:
The branch replaces registry-derived bundled plugin local-media trust with a per-run `trustedLocalMediaToolNames` set from startup plugin metadata, threads it through subscription media filtering, and adds tests plus a changelog entry.

PR surface: Source +90, Tests +38, Docs +1. Total +129 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main imports the plugin registration contract registry in the subscription media helper and uses it to trust bundled plugin local media. I did not run tests because this review is read-only, but the source path and PR test targets are clear.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is indicated: the PR has no blocking findings, carries the protected `maintainer` label, and needs maintainer landing judgment for the local-media trust boundary.

target.title: fix: derive plugin media trust from metadata
target.body:
## Summary

- Remove the subscription-time import of the full plugin registration contract registry.
- Build the per-run trusted local-media tool set from core media-safe tools plus bundled plugin manifest metadata captured before provider schema normalization can clone tools.
- Thread that trusted set through direct media delivery and duplicate suppression, with regression coverage for bundled plugin local media.

Refs #84409.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.subscribeembeddedpisession.test.ts`
- `env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN pnpm check:changed`
- `autoreview --mode local` clean after addressing two accepted findings

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: bundled plugin local `MEDIA:` paths no longer require importing the full plugin registry on subscription paths, and plugin trust is derived from the startup metadata snapshot plus the actual run tool surface.
Real environment tested: local source checkout on macOS, Node/Pnpm repo tooling.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest command above, changed-file gate above, and autoreview local closeout.
Evidence after fix: focused tests passed 5 files / 181 tests; changed gate exited 0; final autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: core media tools retain exact-name local media gating, bundled plugin tools such as `meeting_notes` can pass local media only when included in the trusted run-local set, and duplicate suppression sees the same trusted set.
What was not tested: packaged gateway end-to-end media delivery; this patch is covered by unit/subscription behavior tests plus type/lint/import-cycle gates.
